title: calochortus subalpinus
intro: Calochortus subalpinus, also known as the alpine mariposa lily, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the lily family, characterized by its beautiful white or yellow flowers with distinctive mariposa-shaped markings. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and saponin content. The health benefits of Calochortus subalpinus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activity, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Calochortus subalpinus can be made by infusing the dried roots or flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts as a topical cream or ointment.
